cod: noun. Any of various types of sea fish or the meat from said fish.

con: verb. To study or examine carefully, especially in order to gain knowledge of; to learn, or learn by heart.

cud: noun. The portion of food which is brought back into the mouth by ruminants from their rumen, to be chewed a second time.
cum: preposition. (Should we move, merge or split this sense?) Used in indicating a thing or person which has two or more roles, functions, or
cup: noun. A concave vessel for drinking, usually made of opaque material (as opposed to a glass) and with a handle.
dom: noun. A dominant in sadomasochistic sexual practices.
don: noun. A university professor, particularly one at Oxford or Cambridge.
dump: noun. A place where waste or garbage is left; a ground or place for dumping ashes, refuse, etc.; a disposal site.

dup: verb. to open (a door, gate etc.)

mondo: noun. A dialogue between master and student designed to obtain an intuitive truth.
mono: noun. A bicycle or motorcycle trick where the front wheel is lifted off the ground while riding.
moo: noun. The characteristic lowing sound made by cattle.
mood: noun. A mental or emotional state, composure.
moon: noun. Any natural satellite of a planet.

mound: noun. An artificial hill or elevation of earth; a raised bank; an embankment thrown up for defense

muon: noun. An unstable elementary particle in the lepton family, having similar properties to the electron but with a mass 207 times greater.
nod: verb. To incline the head up and down, as to indicate agreement.

undo: verb. To reverse the effects of an action.

upo: noun. Lagenaria siceraria; a vine grown for its fruit.

oop: interjection. Used to acknowledge a small mistake or accident.

unc: adjective. Old; no longer versed in or relevant to current youth culture.
